patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,7 @@
 # RGE Handoff Document
 
+> **Snapshot**: 2026-06-04 (W08-ACCELERATOR-DOC-RECONCILE — the W08 accelerator-EXECUTION thread is COMPLETE: File/Edit keyboard accelerators now ROUTE through the canonical menu, which is the single source of truth; supersedes the #304 snapshot's "display-only / EXECUTION deferred" line below): #308–#311 land File/Edit accelerator EXECUTION. **#308 (`453a569`, W08-CANONICAL-MENU-SOURCE)** moved the default editor menu into `editor-ui` (`menus::default_menu::default_editor_menu` — the canonical File/Edit/Play/View definition) and added `ResolveResult::command_for_shortcut(&Shortcut) -> Option<&Command>` (an O(1), first-registered-wins index built in `resolve()`), so the host menu bar and editor-shell build from ONE definition with no reverse crate edge. **#309 (`9004b4b`, W08-2-KEYCODE-PARITY)** added the shell-local `lifecycle::accelerator::keycode_to_shortcut` (`rge_input::KeyCode` + Ctrl/Shift → `rge_editor_ui::menus::Shortcut`; it lives in editor-shell because `editor-ui ↛ rge-input`, forbidden-dep rule 4) plus a keyboard/menu PARITY guard — with NO live-path change yet. **#310 (`4fc66cf`, W08-3-KEYSTROKE-CUTOVER)** made the cutover live: `editor-shell`'s `window_event` resolves each un-consumed `KeyDown` via `keycode_to_shortcut → command_for_shortcut` and dispatches the bound `Command` through the shared `EditorShell::route_menu_command` sink — collapsing the former `EditorKeyCommand` Save/Save-As/Undo/Redo arm and the inline `Ctrl+O` arm into one menu-sourced path (and making the old Shift-sloppy `Ctrl+Shift+O` a precise no-op). **#311 (`e98f41d`, W08-4-RETIRE-SHADOW)** retired the now-shadow `EditorKeyCommand::{Undo,Redo,Save,SaveAsProject}` variants — the File/Edit keystroke→command literals live ONLY in the canonical menu now — and re-anchored the parity guard to pin "the menu binds these five accelerators AND `from_key_press` returns None for them" (the invariant became "menu is canonical, no shadow", not "two maps agree"). This SUPERSEDES the #304 snapshot's "**Authority:** mirror the live, executing `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` + the `Ctrl+O` `handle_open_request` arm" framing — the menu is now the canonical SOURCE of that routing (those binds are retired) — and realizes its "the W08 accelerator-EXECUTION work will unify the two" forward-pointer. **STILL DEFERRED** (narrowing #304's "accelerator-table EXECUTION + conflict population" line — only the **File/Edit EXECUTION** half is now shipped): Play/View accelerator display AND execution (Play's real keys are the plain `Space`/`Escape` PIE binds, NOT menu accelerators; View ▸ Reset Camera has no keystroke binding); the `AcceleratorTable` conflict UI / conflict population; the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve (the menu resolves once with `PredicateContext::default()`; the shortcut→command index is static because every `default_editor_menu` entry is unconditional); dynamic toggle LABELS (Play⇄Pause, camera-state-aware View); plugin menu entries; host→shell FIFO menu-click replacement (menu CLICKS still enqueue through the `MenuCommandHandoff` FIFO → `drain_and_route_menu_commands` → `route_menu_command`; W08 unified the KEYBOARD onto that sink, it did NOT replace the click FIFO); generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution beyond the five File/Edit binds. The time-scale binds (`Ctrl+2/0/4`), `Space`/`Escape` playback, and plain-`R` reload remain documented execution-only keybinds with no menu home (an intentional asymmetry retained on `EditorKeyCommand` / the playback + reload axes). The #304 / PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC / A4 / A3 / A2 / A1 snapshots below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.
+
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-04 (MENU-SHORTCUT-DOC-RECONCILE — File/Edit menu items now show their keyboard accelerators, and the menu construction is its own submodule; the menu-deepening thread is closed): #304 (`284998e`, MENU-SHORTCUT-DISPLAY) makes the **File + Edit** menu items render their real keyboard accelerator next to the label — `Open…` Ctrl+O, `Save` Ctrl+S, `Save As New Project…` Ctrl+Shift+S, `Undo` Ctrl+Z, `Redo` Ctrl+Y — via egui's right-aligned `shortcut_text`, sourced from the existing `editor-ui::menus::Shortcut` substrate carried on `MenuEntry.shortcut` (the projection widened from `(label, Command)` to `(label, Option<accelerator-display>, Command)`; a `menu_item` render helper paints each `egui::Button` + optional `shortcut_text`). **Display-only:** clicking still dispatches through the host→shell FIFO — the accelerator is a passive hint, NO command-execution / keystroke-routing / crate-edge change. **File/Edit only (Play/View deferred, `None`):** the shown values mirror the live, executing editor-shell keystroke routing (`EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` + the `Ctrl+O` `handle_open_request` arm), pinned by host tests; the host CANNOT import editor-shell (reverse edge), so `MenuEntry.shortcut` (editor-ui) is the designated home and the W08 accelerator-EXECUTION work will unify the two via the resolved `AcceleratorTable`. Play's only shortcut data is the play-toolbar's ADVISORY `F5/Esc` hints, which don't match the real Space/Escape PIE binds — surfacing them would open the advisory-vs-real accelerator/execution conflict this beat avoids; View ▸ Reset Camera has no binding. **Then #305 (`892a0ad`, EGUIHOST-MENU-EXTRACTION) extracted the menu-construction block** (the four extension-point consts + `build_main_menu_entries` + `play_item_enabled` + `menu_item`) VERBATIM out of `editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` into a new `menu.rs` submodule — behaviour-identical (render path + every test assertion byte-unchanged) — dropping lib.rs 1062 → 836 lines and **RETIRING the `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation** (0 markers crate-wide; mirrors EGUIHOST-TEST-EXTRACTION #301). This narrows the prior snapshot's "accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population" deferred line — the **DISPLAY** half shipped, for **File/Edit**. STILL DEFERRED: accelerator-table EXECUTION + conflict population (Play/View accelerator display deferred with it); the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve; dynamic toggle LABELS; plugin menu entries; generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution (clicks still use the FIFO); dynamic enablement for the File / Edit / View menus. The PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC / A4 / A3 / A2 / A1 snapshots below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.
 
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-03 (PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-DOC-RECONCILE — Play-menu items now grey out per live PlayState; first menu-deepening beat past breadth): #302 (`9960b30`, PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-ENABLE) makes the Play menu items (Play / Pause / Stop / Step) **dynamically enabled** — greyed out (disabled-but-visible) when the current `PlayState` makes the transition a no-op, instead of always-clickable with a benign-swallowed no-op. Authority-clean: `PlayState` stays the sole owner of transition validity via new `can_play` / `can_pause` / `can_stop` / `can_step` queries (derived from the canonical state, pinned to the real `play()` / `pause()` / `stop()` / `step()` transitions by a consistency test); `EditorShell::menu_state_snapshot()` publishes those four booleans each frame through a new latest-only `MenuStateHandoff` (= `Handoff<MenuStateSnapshot>`, the **third** snapshot handoff alongside Inspector / SaveStatus); the host's `render` acquires it and `add_enabled`s each Play item via `play_item_enabled` (a pure boolean router — NO validity rule re-encoded host-side). `MenuStateSnapshot` is a new editor-state **observation aggregator** (sibling to `SaveStatusSnapshot`, NOT a 6th coordination category, so `editor-state-ownership` stays green). **This is a BESPOKE per-frame enablement channel, NOT the W08 registry `Predicate` / per-frame `resolve` path (which remains unbuilt).** Behaviour: Editing → only Play enabled; Playing → Pause + Stop; Paused → all four; File / Edit / View items stay unconditionally enabled (Play-only beat). This narrows the A4 snapshot's "dynamic predicates / per-frame re-resolve (no dynamic toggle labels yet)" deferred line below. STILL DEFERRED: the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame registry re-resolve (menu set/visibility via `Predicate::Closure`); dynamic toggle LABELS (a Play⇄Pause label, a camera-state-aware View item — needs per-frame label re-resolve); accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population; plugin menu entries; generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution (clicks still use the FIFO); and dynamic enablement for the File / Edit / View menus. The A4 / A3 / A2 / A1 snapshots below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.

 2026-06-03 (PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-DOC-RECONCILE — planning-substrate hygiene after the first menu-deepening beat, docs-only). This entry + new top snapshots in `Status.md` / `HANDOFF.md` + a new `plans/BASELINE.md` subsection narrow the A4 reconcile's "dynamic predicates / per-frame re-resolve (no dynamic toggle labels yet)" deferred line. **Bespoke, not W08:** this is a dedicated per-frame enablement channel, NOT the W08 registry `Predicate` / `resolve` path (still unbuilt). **Menu / handoff / snapshot rustdoc already current:** #302 itself (incl. its four stale-prose correction rounds) widened the handoff-set + snapshot-set listings to include the new MenuState members, so this docs reconcile touches NO source comments and NO `docs/§18/ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md` counts. **Still deferred (narrowed):** the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve (menu set/visibility); dynamic toggle labels (Play⇄Pause, camera-state-aware View); accelerator-table execution/display/conflict; plugin entries; generalized registry/accelerator execution (clicks still use the FIFO); dynamic enablement for File / Edit / View (Play-only beat). **History preserved:** the A4 (#300) reconcile entry above + all older dated entries are byte-identical (forward-only). **Scope:** docs-only — edits confined to `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`; no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo / routing / rustdoc / lint-doc change. **Verification:** `git diff --check` clean; `git diff --stat 9960b30..HEAD` = exactly the 4 docs; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0 (`VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`, 10/10 architecture lints 0 violations — Markdown-only change, no Rust touched). **Three required references present in this entry:** `plans/BASELINE.md` new "2026-06-03 — Play-menu items now dynamically enabled per live PlayState (#302)" subsection, the merged PR #302 (`9960b30`), and `ai_handoffs/PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-DOC-RECONCILE_TASK_2026-06-03_23-15-10+0300.md`.

@@ -558,6 +558,32 @@ Until **at least one** of those fires, treat the reflection substrate as observe
 4. Cross-check the editor's call graph against the `CommandBus::submit` / `Action::apply` / `Action::revert` signatures to determine whether user-visible CAD mutations can flow through the existing bus.
 5. Test inventory across `editor-*` (`#[test]` count + integration vs unit breakdown + workflow coverage).
 
+### 2026-06-04 — W08 accelerator EXECUTION shipped: File/Edit keystrokes route through the canonical menu (#308–#311)
+
+**Forward-only follow-up (W08-ACCELERATOR-DOC-RECONCILE).** Narrows the #304/#305 subsection below ("Still open — accelerator-table EXECUTION + conflict population"; "Authority: the shown values mirror the live, executing `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` … the deferred W08 EXECUTION work unifies the two via the resolved `AcceleratorTable`"): the File/Edit accelerator EXECUTION half is now SHIPPED, and the canonical menu is the SOURCE of that routing rather than a mirror of it. Records the four merged PRs that close the W08 accelerator-execution thread.
+
+**Now shipped — File/Edit accelerator EXECUTION via the canonical menu (#308–#311).**
+- **#308 (`453a569`, W08-CANONICAL-MENU-SOURCE)** moved the default editor menu into `editor-ui` (`menus::default_menu::default_editor_menu` — the File/Edit/Play/View definition) and added `ResolveResult::command_for_shortcut(&Shortcut) -> Option<&Command>` (an O(1), first-registered-wins index built in `resolve()`). Both the host menu bar and `editor-shell` build from this one definition; no reverse crate edge.
+- **#309 (`9004b4b`, W08-2-KEYCODE-PARITY)** added the shell-local `lifecycle::accelerator::keycode_to_shortcut` (`rge_input::KeyCode` + Ctrl/Shift → `rge_editor_ui::menus::Shortcut`; it lives in editor-shell because `editor-ui ↛ rge-input`, forbidden-dep rule 4) plus a parity guard locking the editor-shell keyboard map to the canonical menu — NO live-path change.
+- **#310 (`4fc66cf`, W08-3-KEYSTROKE-CUTOVER)** made the cutover live: `window_event` resolves each un-consumed `KeyDown` via `keycode_to_shortcut → command_for_shortcut` and dispatches the bound `Command` through the shared `EditorShell::route_menu_command` sink (the same one the host→shell menu FIFO drains into). This collapsed the former `EditorKeyCommand` Save/Save-As/Undo/Redo arm and the inline `Ctrl+O` arm into one menu-sourced path; the old Shift-sloppy `key == KeyO && ctrl` check became the precise menu bind, so `Ctrl+Shift+O` is now a no-op.
+- **#311 (`e98f41d`, W08-4-RETIRE-SHADOW)** retired the now-shadow `EditorKeyCommand::{Undo, Redo, Save, SaveAsProject}` variants (the File/Edit keystroke→command literals live ONLY in the canonical menu now) and re-anchored the parity guard to pin "the menu binds the five File/Edit accelerators AND `from_key_press` returns None for them" — the invariant became "menu is canonical, no shadow", not "two maps agree". `EditorKeyCommand` survives for the execution-only time-scale binds (`Ctrl+2/0/4`).
+
+**Authority (updated).** `editor-ui::menus::default_editor_menu` is the single source of truth for the File/Edit accelerators; editor-shell resolves keystrokes through it (`keycode_to_shortcut → command_for_shortcut → route_menu_command`). The #304 subsection's "Authority: mirror the live `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` + the `Ctrl+O` `handle_open_request` arm" is superseded — those were the shadow map, now retired.
+
+**Still open — explicitly NOT closed here (the File/Edit accelerator EXECUTION half above IS closed):**
+- Play/View accelerator display AND execution. Play's real keys are the plain `Space`/`Escape` PIE binds (not menu accelerators); View ▸ Reset Camera has no keystroke binding.
+- the `AcceleratorTable` conflict UI / conflict population surface.
+- the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve (the menu resolves once with `PredicateContext::default()`; the shortcut→command index is static because every `default_editor_menu` entry is unconditional).
+- dynamic toggle LABELS (Play⇄Pause, camera-state-aware View).
+- plugin menu entries.
+- host→shell FIFO menu-click replacement — menu CLICKS still enqueue through the `MenuCommandHandoff` FIFO (`drain_and_route_menu_commands → route_menu_command`); W08 unified the KEYBOARD onto that sink, it did NOT replace the click FIFO.
+- generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution beyond the five File/Edit binds.
+- the time-scale binds (`Ctrl+2/0/4`), `Space`/`Escape` playback, and plain-`R` reload remain documented execution-only keybinds with no menu home (an intentional asymmetry retained on `EditorKeyCommand` / the playback + reload axes).
+
+**Historical preservation.** The #304/#305 (File/Edit accelerator display) subsection below and all earlier dated entries are preserved byte-identical; their "accelerator-table EXECUTION … Still open" + "mirror the live `EditorKeyCommand` routing" lines are narrowed forward by this subsection (File/Edit EXECUTION shipped; the menu is now the source), not rewritten in place.
+
+**Scope:** docs only (`Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + `plans/BASELINE.md` + `change.md`); no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo change (the accelerator/menu rustdoc was made current inside #308–#311 themselves).
+
 ### 2026-06-04 — File/Edit menu accelerators shown; menu construction extracted to a submodule (#304 + #305)
